jd_text: ASMO is a groundbreaking joint venture between DHL and Aramco. Inheriting DHL’s logistics excellence and Aramco’s extensive supply chain ecosystem, we are here to set a new benchmark and redefine the procurement and supply chain landscape, enabling growth. ASMO aims to be operational in 2025 and provide reliable end-to-end integrated procurement and supply chain services for companies across the industrial, energy, chemical, and petrochemical sectors. Our focus customers in the short term will be Saudi Aramco and its Affiliates. In the long term, all the industrial sectors within Saudi Arabia aim to reach the MENA region. Objective The role holder is plays a critical role in determining the required materials and generating purchase orders and ensuring materials meet specifications, quality standards, and are cost-efficient. The role holder is also responsible for coordinating with other departments regarding PO tracking, timelines, supplier payments, etc. In addition, the role holder is responsible for scheduling and overseeing the supply and delivery of materials and products. General Responsibilities ▪ Contribute to the development and execution of the Department short to mid-term strategic plan to ensure alignment with the Department’s strategic priorities, and the corporate goals. ▪ Contribute to the development of annual business plans for the assigned areas of responsibility to support the achievement of financial and strategic objectives. ▪ Monitor, control and report key performance indicators related to the Department to track performance and recommend corrective actions Operational ▪ Control materials requirement planning processes to ensure accurate and timely procurement of materials. ▪ Monitor and analyze material consumption patterns to optimize inventory levels and reduce stockouts. ▪ Monitor master data and parameters accuracy and identify updates/refinement needed. Collaborate with relevant cross functional teams (e.g. MDM, IT,…) to ensure necessary updates are implemented ▪ Coordinate with suppliers to ensure timely delivery of materials to meet stocking requirements. ▪ Establish and maintain safety stock levels based on inventory norms for stocked items to prevent stockouts ▪ Coordinate with the logistics teams for any expedite requests in case of stockouts/low stock ▪ Maintain accurate records of materials usage, inventory levels, and procurement activities. ▪ Conduct regular analysis and reporting of materials planning performance metrics. ▪ Identify and implement process improvements to enhance materials requirement planning efficiency and accuracy. ▪ Collaborate with cross-functional teams to provide solutions to their procurement requirements. ▪ Stay updated on industry trends and best practices in materials requirement planning. ▪ Manage the activities of direct reports to ensure that all work is carried out in an efficient manner, in line with the annual business plans, policies and procedures. ▪ Manage the performance management process by setting annual objectives, and developing staff through mentoring, coaching and constructive feedback. ▪ Identify employees’ training and developmental needs and in conjunction with Human Resources Department bridge the knowledge gaps to ensure continuous progression, and high-level of competence within the team. ▪ Contribute to defining an optimum structure for the Department, to ensure smooth workflow of operational processes and optimal utilisation of resources. Financial Accountability ▪ Provide input into the preparation and consolidation of the Department’s budget, monitor financial performance during the year and identify areas of unsatisfactory performance (if any), and recommend mitigating actions Change Management ▪ Identify and manage implementation of change initiatives on systems, processes and practices considering ‘international leading practices’, and/or other changes in the business environment. Policies, Systems, Processes & Procedures ▪ Recommend improvements to departmental procedures, and ensure implementation of policies, procedures, systems, and controls covering the assigned areas of responsibility so that all activities meet ASMO’s standards, compliance, and regulatory requirements, align to business requirements, and increase operational efficiency. ▪ Identify opportunities for the continuous improvement of systems, processes and practices considering ‘international leading practices’, in order to increase operational effectiveness. ▪ Act as the functional owner for relevant systems/modules and collaborate with cross functional representatives (e.g. IT, MDM,…) to identify business requirements and provide support for system enablement Management Reporting ▪ Present regular and ad-hoc management reports related to the function, provide recommendations to the Category Cluster Director on new opportunities, highlight critical issues and challenges, and provide strategic insight to ensure effective decision-making. Qualification ▪ Bachelor's degree in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Economics, or a related field from a recognized and accredited university is required ▪ Master's degree in Supply Chain Management, Business Administration, Economics, or a related field from a recognized and accredited university is Prefferd Professional Certifications ▪ APICS Certified in Production and Inventory Management (CPIM) ▪ Certified Professional in Inventory Management (CPIM) ▪ Certified in Logistics, Transportation, and Distribution (CLTD) Language Skills ▪ Demonstrated proficiency in oral and written English ▪ 5 years’ experience in similar or related field Deadline: 30 days from posting
